title: How to fix facial asymmetry with Botox.
filename: How to fix facial asymmetry with Botox..en.vtt
excerpt: How to fix facial asymmetry with botl toxins this video is going to cover how to do this with your patients I think using bot toxins correct asymmetry is both under and sometimes overused with your patients because there are times when it is amazing and other times when it's just…

segments.text: how to fix facial asymmetry with botl toxins this video is going to cover how to do this with your patients I think using bot toxins correct asymmetry is both under and sometimes overused with your patients because there are times when it is amazing and other times when it's just not the right thing to do so let's have a look at the different parts of the face where you can use neurom modulators to increase symmetry probably the most common area that patients will ask will be with eyebrows if you have one eyebrow that is slightly lower than the other people notice this because they frame your face and the request to see if you can get them the same is quite common now this can both work out really well but can also work out quite badly and I'll share the first experience I ever had treating a patient with an asymmetrical eyebrow was quite complicated because she had trauma to her forehead so she had injured her forehead at some point in her life and she had no movement on one side and this caused her a lot of distress because she had one eyebrow that was actually quite raised and the other one that was completely dropped and she came asking

me to look more symmetrical and I delivered exactly what she asked for I relaxed the muscle on the side that was too lifted and she looked symmetrical but she absolutely hated the result and the reason for that is because she went from looking alert and interested even though she was asymmetrical to looking exhausted and uninterested and it's really interesting to think about patients in this way one of my messages I hope many of you get from listening to my channel is it's really important to think about the face as a tool for interaction ING with other people not just as something that's a picture that needs to become beautiful in a still image so what she had noticed as a result of her treatment was she preferred the function of her face over the form it's better to be able to look interested and engaged with people even if you look very asymmetrical the key Point here is remember that facial symmetry is not the be and Endor yes we've all heard it since we were small children that symmetry is beautiful but it's actually not all there is to it and many patients will prefer a function face um with DM in it then they will a

perfectly symmetrical face if they lose something else let's now look at what you can do to adjust eyebrows the case that I was faced with was hard because there was no way I was ever going to get much of a lift but if you have more elevation activity so the frontales is more active there is something you can do to adjust eyebrows but there's very little you can do that doesn't risk then increasing asymmetry in a different way there are really just three injection points that you can do with confidence and the rest you can do but you've got to think a lot harder about it if you remember how to lift an eyebrow generally the first step and the easiest way to lift an eyebrow is just to put maybe four units at the tail of the eyebrow and this will release the strongest part of orbicularis oculi as it's pulling down the eyebrow and allow for a small lift and this is the easiest thing you can do if the patient wants nothing except brow asymmetry you can do that and it's very unlikely they will spot anything else because the orbicular oculi is still working everywhere else but just at this point it's weaker and that allows a little bit of lift in the eyebrow the Step Beyond that is to treat underneath the eyebrow so you're still treating orbicularis oculi and you're

very superficial to keep the eyelid safe you're over the periosteum you're over the bone of the orbit but you can treat maybe two to three units underneath the tail of the eyebrow and this releases even more of that muscle allowing the eyebrow laterally to lift more the third thing you can do requires a lot more thought and this is around adjusting the amount of the frontalis muscle that is untreated on one side versus the other side and this is also a treatment that can cause different a symmetries CU you end up with lines on the side that you're trying to lift and no lines on the side that you're not trying to lift and this obviously causes a different issue and it's very hard to get it exactly accurate but if you want to try it's all about the amount of muscle that you leave untreated so if you remember the shape that I so often draw in patients when I'm teaching this which is around how much of the muscle is treated so we Mark out an area where we don't mind treating we do we draw the line we create the vectors that you've seen many times the size of this triangle that is untreated can be adjusted to give you more lift on one side and less lift on the other essentially the principle is have less

treated muscle on the side you want to lift and if you make smaller adjustments you can sometimes get this right but the downside is you might get an equal lift and more of a lift on one side but then you have lines and then you got to solve that with a different problem that so that's why that's the third option is because it sometimes causes problems later on that then need a different treatment to solve them either more toxin or maybe end up treating with filler but it's a little bit trickier than the simpler versions so that's the eyebrow probably the next most common reason that I'm asked to treat asymmetrical faces is actually with quite a rare thing which is Bell's pausy so when you have Bell's pausy and it persists you get a weakening or a decrease in tone on one half of the face and an increase in tone on the other half of the face and it's actually treating the unaffected side of the face that gets you a more symmetrical result so where the face is hyperactive and the tone is high you can use a small amount of Bim toxin strategically and that actually balances out the tone across the face and the results can be really amazing this can be a fantastic result but you have to think very carefully

about which muscles are hyp dnamic if you ever have a patient with Bell's py try and film them moving and see where you see the additional activity happening and you'll see one ey is more active you'll see a more of a downturn usually at the Dao and these are the muscles that with small amount of toxin you can treat them and cause an increase in Symmetry and balance probably the most effective one I think is when they have Dao hyperactivity so they one side when they smile that actually pulls their mouth down so this is obviously the opposite of what you're trying to do when you're smiling this is why a small amount of toxin here makes such a lovely difference to the patient because they can suddenly then SM smile normally I would do it as a normal Doo treatment but just be very careful about the dose that you use first time maybe start with a lower dose and work your way up because it's not a normal Dao treatment we're not trying to neutralize it we're just trying to take away some of that resting tone and the hyperactivity on that side so one to two units in the Dio should improve symmetry when the down Pull is so obvious the next area

where you might see asymmetry is in the master muscle um often you get one side that seems to be bigger than the other um this is quite tricky because it's quite hard to get this exactly right but I would simply do a treatment depending on the relative size of the muscle I would start with a lower dose than I would normally for master because I'm not trying to shrink it to its maximum relaxed state I'm just trying to cause atrophy that makes it metrical so if you're use usually using sort of 24 to 50 units you can start with less you know 20 units 15 units and then get the patient back in 6 weeks time learn about that response and then over time adjust it so that it is eventually getting more and more symmetrical of course the other approach is if you think both Masters are hypertrophied but one side is particularly strong you can actually just neutralize both of them because they should return to a similar size because um both of them are completely relaxed in that lower third so that's another way of thinking about it which is you don't always have to do asymmetrical treatments in order to increase symmetry because if you're

taking both sides back to a more neutral state where they're both effectively atrophied as much as they can be then you're going to be more symmetrical unless you're underlying asymmetry is bone which does occasionally happen but I'd say mostly when I've seen it it's more likely muscle another asymmetry which you may occasionally see and you've probably seen already in Elvis is overactivity of the levator labii leasi muscle on just one side so if you have a snar that's stronger on one side and you wanted to get rid of it if you want to not look like the king of rock and roll then one unit on just one side is a good first pass you can always add more it's very easy to go too far and cause the other problem a different asymmetry and that's why small doses are the Hallmark of correcting asymmetry and finally it's a really good test of your own facial knowledge to try and solve a facial asymmetry problem even if you don't treat but really think through what you would do to increase Symmetry and what the potential downsides or the ways that you may cause an additional asymmetrical result through overtreating a particular

area and this is also a good thing to place on forums if you have a support network put that into the network with your treatment plan and see what other people have to say because it really makes you think carefully about how all these muscles work so I recommend you think through it even if you decide not to do the treatment so I hope that's helped you understand the anatomy much more deeply remember I've got much more coming on this click on the link and sign up for the waiting list for my new Anatomy [Music] course
